title: Lesson 22 — Growth and Decay

words.definition: to begin to grow or increase rapidly; to flourish
words.example: The city's suburbs burgeoned almost overnight.

words.definition: to increase rapidly in number; to multiply
to grow or develop in a healthy, vigorous way; to thrive
words.example: The little business flourished after the move downtown.

words.definition: to grow, develop, or prosper well
words.example: Ferns thrive in cool, damp shade.

words.definition: in an initial stage; beginning to appear or develop
words.example: An incipient crisis, still small enough to stop.

words.definition: in a very early stage of development
words.example: The idea was still embryonic, barely a rough sketch.
A fledgling airline that owned just two planes.

words.definition: to increase in size, strength, or intensity
words.example: Enthusiasm waxed and waned over the long campaign.

words.definition: to decrease in size, strength, or intensity
words.example: Public interest waned as the months dragged on.

words.definition: to diminish gradually in size, amount, or strength
words.example: Their savings dwindled to almost nothing.

words.definition: to waste away, especially from disuse or neglect

words.example: The flowers withered in the relentless heat.

story: In its first spring the little venture seemed to [[burgeon|burgeon]]. Orders [[proliferate|proliferated]], the team [[flourish|flourished]], and morale [[thrive|thrived]]. What had been an [[incipient|incipient]], almost [[embryonic|embryonic]] idea grew into a real, if [[fledgling|fledgling]], company. But fortunes [[wax|wax]] and [[wane|wane]]. When a far bigger rival arrived, interest began to [[dwindle|dwindle]]; the once-busy floor fell quiet, ambition seemed to [[atrophy|atrophy]], and the bright plans of March slowly [[wither|withered]] by the fall.
